title: Cast your vote on new euro banknote design proposals
content: Have your say on the ten shortlisted design proposals for the next series of euro banknotes that have been unveiled by the European Central Bank. An online survey where you can give your feedback is open until 21 September. The winning proposal will be selected around the end of the year.

title: A renewed commitment to protect and empower the EU's citizens
content: The European Pillar of Social Rights helps to improve people's lives through better jobs, equal opportunities, quality working conditions and strong social protection. A communication has been published that looks at its impact and highlights three areas which require urgent additional attention.

title: A plan to make Europe the first electro-continent
content: The Commission has put forward the electrification action plan as part of the EU’s wider efforts to boost Europe’s competitiveness, decarbonisation and independence. The plan introduces measures to make electricity more competitive and to incentivise the use of electricity-based technologies.

title: Rule of law report strengthens EU democracy and fundamental rights
content: The 2026 Rule of Law report shows how well the rule of law is holding up across the EU and 4 potential EU members: Albania, Montenegro, North Macedonia and Serbia. It finds that, while some challenges persist, there is continued progress in many EU countries, with many reforms completed or underway.

title: Venezuela earthquakes: EU provides €20 million in humanitarian funding
content: The EU is providing €20 million in humanitarian funding to help the most affected communities in Venezuela, following the recent earthquakes. Half of the funding will support the search and rescue, medical and other expert teams' operations, along with in-kind assistance deployed in the country.

title: Safer cars, safer roads: New rules take effect
content: Starting on 7 July 2026, all newly registered cars and vans in the EU will be subject to more advanced safety requirements. This is the latest phase in the rollout of EU vehicle safety rules under the General Safety Regulation, and will contribute to making Europe's roads safer.

title: New plan to boost resilience and prosperity of livestock farming
content: A new EU strategy for the livestock sector sets out actions to help farmers address current economic, environmental and market challenges. Based on five priority areas, including sustainability and competitiveness, the strategy aims to make the livestock sector more prosperous and resilient overall.

title: Wildfires: EU sends emergency support to Portugal and France
content: Both Portugal and France requested civil protection assistance from the EU last weekend following devastating local wildfires. In response, the EU mobilised several firefighting aircraft, ground teams and vehicles from Sweden, Cyprus, Italy and Spain to help emergency crews on the ground.

title: Use of plastic packaging material on the rise in the EU
content: A new EU study has found that plastic is the only packaging material that is on the rise, growing by 11 percent between 2011 and 2025. The study looked at the use of all packaging materials including plastic, glass, paper and metals in 19 EU countries during this period.

title: International Plastic Bag Free Day – from single use to reuse
content: To raise awareness about eliminating plastic bag use worldwide and promote eco-friendly reusable alternatives, we observe International Plastic Bag Free Day every 3 July. On this occasion, take a look at how EU rules are helping to drastically reduce the consumption and use of plastics.

title: Earthquakes in Venezuela: EU deploys emergency assistance
content: The Commission is deploying rescue teams and other emergency assistance to Venezuela following the strong earthquakes on 24 June. Eight EU countries are sending assistance through the EU civil protection mechanism. The Copernicus satellite service has also been activated to support the response.

title: New proposals to improve fight against cross-border crime and terrorism
content: The European Commission has proposed measures to strengthen the EU’s response to an evolving criminal landscape that is becoming more sophisticated, international and digital. The capacities of EU agencies Europol and Eurojust will be reinforced to better fight crime and better protect EU citizens.

title: Time for a swim: EU bathing waters remain clean
content: According to the latest EU bathing water report, 85% of Europe’s bathing sites meet ‘excellent’ standards. Austria, Bulgaria, Cyprus and Greece have the highest share for excellent quality bathing waters. EU laws on water have played a significant role in improving bathing water quality.
snippet: According to the latest EU bathing water report, 85% of Europe’s bathing sites meet ‘excellent’ standards. Austria, Bulgaria, Cyprus and Greece have the highest share for excellent quality bathing

title: New report highlights humanitarian toll of potential El Niño occurrence
content: A new EU report sets out the potential impact of the predicted El Niño climate phenomenon later this year. It predicts unusually warm weather to spread across much of the world, including in Europe, while East Africa, parts of Asia, and large parts of South and North America will get more rainfall.
